items.text: We face substantial industry competition, including impacts from technological disruption and automation adoption.
items.rationale: Restates competitive risk but adds automation/technological disruption as a new competitive factor, expanding the existing competition risk framing.

items.text: The industry is undergoing rapid technological change, including the emergence of disruptive technologies and accelerated adoption of automation and AI.
items.rationale: Expands on industry competition risk by adding detail about technological change and AI/automation adoption trends.

items.text: Competitors are leveraging advanced digital platforms, AI-driven freight matching, and automation to improve efficiency and reduce costs.
items.rationale: Provides specific detail on how competitors use AI and automation, elaborating the existing competitive risk rather than introducing a wholly new risk.

items.text: If we fail to maintain the pace, scale, or quality of automation and AI adoption, we may be unable to achieve our strategic goals for operational efficiency and digital transformation.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific new risk tied to the company's own failure to adopt automation/AI at sufficient pace, scale, or quality, affecting strategic goals.

items.text: Inability to keep up with these advancements could increase our cost to serve customers, reduce productivity and negatively impact our ability to compete.
items.rationale: Elaborates the consequences of failing to keep up with technology, building on the newly introduced automation/AI risk.

items.text: Delays in implementing new systems or integrating emerging technologies into our workflows may also lead to higher operating expenses and missed opportunities for growth.
items.rationale: Further detail on operational consequences of technology adoption delays, continuing the same risk theme.

items.text: If we cannot effectively respond to competitive pressures and technological disruption, our business, financial condition, and results of operations could be materially and adversely affected.
items.rationale: Generic concluding statement about competitive/technological risk without new specific content.

items.text: Our business outside of the United States is subject to various risks, including: • changes in tariffs, trade restrictions, trade agreements, and taxations.
items.rationale: Standard list-style disclosure of general international business risks including tariffs, common boilerplate language.

items.text: In 2025, the United States government made significant changes to our national trade policy, including imposing tariffs on certain goods imported into the United States.
items.rationale: Introduces a specific factual development: new 2025 U.S. tariffs on imported goods, a concrete new risk driver.

items.text: The tariffs impacted our Global Forwarding business in 2025, most significantly in the second quarter of 2025, with volatile market conditions causing global demand fluctuations and lower volumes.
items.rationale: Provides specific, quantifiable impact of tariffs on the Global Forwarding segment in 2025, a new substantive disclosure.

items.text: Changes in United States trade policy, including tariffs on certain imported goods, could continue to increase our costs and disrupt global supply chains.
items.rationale: Generalizes the tariff risk into ongoing trade policy risk, expanding on the newly disclosed tariff issue.

items.text: These actions, and any retaliatory measures by other countries, may lead to higher transportation costs and reduced demand, resulting in potential loss of freight volume.
items.rationale: Further elaborates potential consequences (retaliation, higher costs, lost volume) of the tariff risk already introduced.

items.text: Additionally, heightened customs requirements could delay shipments and require significant internal resources, increasing operating expenses and negatively impacting our ability to serve customers efficiently.
items.rationale: Details additional operational impacts (customs delays, increased expenses) stemming from the same tariff/trade risk.

items.text: We leverage machine learning and AI technologies to enhance operational efficiency, automate processes, and improve the customer experience across our logistics platform.
items.rationale: Describes use of AI/ML positively for efficiency and customer experience, not a risk disclosure.

items.text: Department of Agriculture (“USDA”) as required by Perishable Agricultural Commodities Act (“PACA”).
items.rationale: Reference to regulatory body/act (USDA/PACA) is factual/regulatory context, not a new or expanded risk.
